content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as a population of nested, semi-autonomous curiosity-driven sub-agents that collectively vote on which unresolved environmental patterns are worth querying for human labels, rather than relying on centralized uncertainty scoring for active selection. This nested sub-agent architecture leverages the diverse exploratory drives of individual sub-agents to surface more high-impact unlabeled data than traditional single-branch active learning methods.
